pipeline_mode.rationale: Research task with conditional implementation — adaptive mode allows early termination if research concludes NO-GO

jit.enabled.rationale: JIT is not needed — this is a self-contained research+refactor task with no external dependencies or long-running operations

replanning.enabled.rationale: Essential for this task — if Phase 2 research synthesis concludes NO-GO, the pipeline should replan to skip Phases 5-7 (TDD, Plan, Execute) and go directly to Phase 8-9 (Verify findings, Complete)

replanning.max_cycles.rationale: One replan is sufficient — the key decision point is after Phase 2 (GO vs NO-GO)

fast_track.enabled.rationale: Not eligible — multi-file scope, open-ended research question, ambiguity score 4/5

meta_agent.confidence_threshold.rationale: Composite confidence is 0.65 (below threshold), but this is expected for research tasks — ambiguity is the task, not a blocker

routing.enabled.rationale: No single skill can handle this — requires full research pipeline

verification.dimension_weights.rationale: Security weight stays minimal (prompt files only, no code execution). Correctness and spec_alignment are equally important — content preservation is critical for a format migration. Robustness covers edge cases in large files.

skip_phases.rationale: All phases needed. Research (1-2) is the core deliverable. Brainstorm (3) provides multi-perspective evaluation. Spec (4) defines changes. TDD (5) ensures quality. Plan (6) sequences work. Execute (7) implements. Verify (8) confirms. Complete (9) reports.

approval_gates.rationale: Gate after brainstorm (3): confirm the GO/NO-GO direction. Gate after spec (4): approve exact changes before TDD and implementation. Gate after plan (6): confirm implementation order before execution.

phase_specific_notes.phase_1: Research must produce quantitative token measurements on actual files, not estimates. Read at least 3 representative files per category.
phase_specific_notes.phase_2: Synthesis must produce a clear GO/NO-GO/PARTIAL-GO per file category. If overall NO-GO, flag for replanning to skip implementation phases.
phase_specific_notes.phase_3: The Token Economist and Prompt Quality Advocate will likely disagree. The Pragmatic Maintainer is the tiebreaker for 'is it worth the migration cost?'
phase_specific_notes.phase_4: If GO: spec must include before/after examples using REAL file content. If NO-GO: spec should still document minor improvements.
phase_specific_notes.phase_5: Tests must work with bash built-ins only — no jq, no yq, no python. The test harness is pure shell.
phase_specific_notes.phase_6: Plan smallest category first (core, 11 files) as validation, then configs (8), then agents (21), then skills (28 — if changing at all).
phase_specific_notes.phase_7: Run tests/harness/run-tests.sh after EACH file category migration, not just at the end.
phase_specific_notes.phase_8: Content preservation is the #1 verification priority. Count information units (steps, constraints, fields) in original vs migrated.
phase_specific_notes.phase_9: Report in Czech (user's language) with English technical terms. Reference specific token measurements from Phase 1.
